templates.summary: Create an associative polar array (ARRAYPOLAR) from the drawing's last-created entity, using explicit center, item count, angle, and rotate prompts.

center_point.description: Center point of the polar array as a single 'x,y' pair -- AutoCAD's 'specify center point' prompt consumes ONE comma-joined line (feeding x/y/z as separate script lines mis-lands on later prompts; measured live, cert wave 1).

item_count.description: Total number of items in the polar array, including the source object. Answered DIRECTLY at '항목 수 입력 [사이의 각도(A)/표현식(E)] <4>:' -- there is no 'I' option letter at this stage (measured live, cert wave 2).

angle_to_fill.description: Fill angle in degrees, answered DIRECTLY at '채울 각도 지정(+=ccw, -=cw) 또는 [표현식(EX)] <360>:' (no option letter; measured live, cert wave 2).

templates.notes: v1 scope limitation (like define.arrayrect): selects via AutoCAD's 'L' (Last) option; rows/levels/rotate stay at command defaults. Rebuilt from live stdout (cert wave 2, three measured iterations): the headless flow is sequential value prompts -- center point (one comma-joined line), item count (direct integer), fill angle (direct number) -- FOLLOWED by the grip-edit option menu ('그립을 선택하여 배열을 편집하거나 [...] <종료>:'), which the trailing 'X' exits. The old 'I'/'A' option letters do not exist at the value-prompt stages on this Korean-locale build.
